behaviors.description: Review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file
behaviors.justification: This tests diff parsing logic—a deterministic operation that extracts changed line ranges from a GitHub diff format. The failure is in-process: off-by-one errors, incorrect range extraction, or accidentally including full file content. A mock diff string (no I/O) is sufficient to catch these logic errors. We're not testing GitHub's API response; we're testing our parsing of it.
behaviors.test_description: Create a mock GitHub diff with multiple files, each with specific changed line ranges (e.g., file A lines 10–15, file B lines 30–35). Pass this to the diff parsing logic and verify only the changed ranges are extracted and sent to the review agent, not the full file content. Assert that analysis input contains exactly the changed line portions.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request
behaviors.justification: Positioning comments correctly requires validating that the GitHub API call is formatted with the right position parameter. The failure is either: wrong line number calculation (caught by mocking the GitHub API and validating request format) or API behavior change (caught by integration testing). A pure mock can catch the first; an integration test catches both. Multiple modules must work together: diff parsing, issue identification at a location, and GitHub API call construction.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the GitHub API for comment posting. Run the agent on a PR diff with known issues at specific line positions (e.g., a bug at diff line 42). Verify that the agent POSTs to GitHub with the correct position parameter (line 42, not 41 or 43) using the correct syntax for the GitHub API version in use. Assert the mocked API receives the correctly formatted request with matching line numbers and position field.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug
behaviors.justification: Defect identification depends on the LLM's reasoning capability, which is non-deterministic. A mocked LLM that always returns 'bug found' would pass the test but wouldn't verify the real system. The failure mode is: we run the test 100 times with real code and a real bug, and 20% of the time the LLM misses it, misclassifies it, or describes it incorrectly. Only calling the real LLM catches this.
behaviors.test_description: Create or select a real PR (or realistic PR content) containing a known introduced bug—e.g., missing null check before method call, uncaught exception in a new code path, off-by-one error in array access. Call the agent with the real PR diff and let it run the actual LLM-based identification logic. Verify that at least one finding is identified that correctly describes the introduced defect. Run the test multiple times to account for LLM non-determinism and ensure the bug is reliably detected.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted—no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment
behaviors.justification: This tests the consistency of the bot's internal orchestration: the mapping between identified findings and posted comments. The failure is a logic bug where the summary lists finding X but no inline comment was posted for X (or vice versa). This requires mocking GitHub API calls to verify both the count and content of inline and summary comments posted, and to check their correspondence. Multiple subsystems (identification, comment posting, summary generation) must be wired together correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the GitHub API for posting comments (inline and top-level). Run the agent on a PR containing multiple known issues. After execution, verify: (1) the count of inline comments posted equals the count of findings in the summary, (2) for each finding in the summary, exactly one inline comment was posted with matching content/reference, (3) no inline comments were posted without a corresponding summary finding. Use mock call assertions to verify the actual posting calls made.

behaviors.description: The full sequence—reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment—completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention
behaviors.justification: This is the end-to-end pipeline test that catches failures only visible when all real systems work together. Individual component tests might pass while the integrated system fails due to: real LLM token limits on large diffs, GitHub API rate limiting, authentication failures, network timeouts, or unexpected PR format. The failure is 'the system fails in production' rather than 'this one component is wrong.' Requires real LLM (Agentic), real GitHub (System), and the full sequence orchestrated together (Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Select a real test PR in a test GitHub repository containing intentional code issues. Run the full agent pipeline end-to-end: fetch the PR diff from real GitHub, call the real LLM to identify defects, POST inline comments to the real PR, POST a summary comment. Verify all steps complete without errors or timeouts. Confirm that comments are visible on the real PR and the bot completed without manual intervention or partial failure.
